hero.title: A living space for art, research, and collective imagination.
hero.subtitle: A living space for art, research, and collective imagination.

about.title: About
about.body: Gihanga Institute of Contemporary Art (GICA) in Kigali is an independent non-profit art institute dedicated to contemporary art and to fostering critical exchange. Spanning 777 sqm, GICA was designed by Rwandan architect Amin Gafaranga and co-founded by Kami Gahiga and Kaneza Schaal. The institute encompasses a range of contemporary art practices including visual art, performance, film and literature, and is commmitted to supporting artists working in Rwanda and across the region.
about.mission: GICA' mission is to provide access to contemporary art in Rwanda and the region and to position the region within global artistic and intellectual conversations. Our vision is to establish Rwanda as a leading hub for contemporary art and critical exchange.
about.gihanga: (noun) a legendary figure often regarded as the founding hero and first king of Rwanda, Gihanga is credited in Rwandan mythology and oral tradition with introducing the essential cultural traditions, technologies, and practices that shaped Rwandan society, symbolising the link between historical inheritance and contemporary creation.
about.symbol: The institute's symbol is inspired by the golden crested crown of the Grey Crowned Crane, the emblematic bird native to the Great Lakes region. Our symbol represents artistic vision, awakening, and cultural unity.

quotes.text: This is a huge contribution to Rwanda's growing cultural infrastructure and creative industry.
quotes.author: Jean-Guy Africa

quotes.text: What a brilliant and pioneering idea. It is a timely and truly needed space.
quotes.author: Dr.Salah Hassan

quotes.text: GICA's inauguration marks a very special moment for the country's cultural arts, its platform makes the arts more accessible to all.
quotes.author: Hon. Sandrine Umutoni
quotes.company: Minister, State for Youth and the Arts, Rwanda

quotes.text: It is an amazing new initiative for Rwanda, it is a platform that I really believe is going to change the impact of art and culture in the region.
quotes.author: Touria El Glaoui
quotes.company: Founding Director, 1-54 Contemporary African Art Fair (London, Marrakech, New York)

quotes.text: A pillar, a magnet and a hub for life.
quotes.author: Saana Gateja

quotes.text: What a gift for Kigali, for Rwanda, Africa and the world.
quotes.author: Caroline Greyling
quotes.company: Director, Noval Foundation, Cape Town, South Africa

quotes.text: Art is a basic language and this space will give more visibility to artists from Rwanda and beyond. It creates hope for the next generation of artists, offering them a platform where they can build their careers. It's an immense addition to the cultural landscape of Rwanda.
quotes.author: Othman Lazraq
quotes.company: Founder, Museum of Contemporary African Art Al Maaden (MACAAL)

partners.title: Partners
partners.subtitle: We would like to thank the following individuals and organizations who have supported us along the way...

visit_page.hero_subtitle: Plan your visit and experience contemporary art in Kigali.
visit_page.booking_paragraph: We highly encourage you to book your visit in advance to ensure a smooth experience and avoid waiting in line. Walk-ins are welcome, subject to capacity.
visit_page.booking_note:
Takes less than 1 minute.
Group visits larger than 5
are welcome to contact us
ahead of time.

faq_default_0.a: Yes. GICA is currently free to the public.

faq_default_1.q: Do I need to book in advance?
faq_default_1.a: No booking is required for general visits. Some talks, screenings, or workshops may require prior registration, which will be available via our website.

faq_default_2.a: Tuesday - Sunday. 11:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Closed on Mondays)

faq_default_3.a: KN 14 St 28, Kimihurura, Kigali, Rwanda. Pedestrian access is via the small walkway facing the 14th Avenue. Parking access is available via Boho and 14th Avenue, only drop-off in front of GICA.

faq_default_4.a: The ground floor is accessible. The first floor and lower ground floor are accessed via stairs. If you require accessibility support, please contact us in advance and we will do our best to accommodate your visit.
